blurb: The transfiguration offers a glimpse of Christ's true glory—not as something earned through spiritual discipline, but as the radiant reality that was always his. Peter's impulse to build memorials reveals our human tendency to try to contain and domesticate what ought to transform us, to turn the luminous moment into religious real estate. But divine glory cannot be housed in human structures; it can only be received in the silence of awestruck hearts that finally understand they are standing on holy ground.

verses.text: And it came to pass about an eight days after these sayings, he took Peter and John and James, and went up into a mountain to pray.
And as he prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white, and glistering.
And, behold, there talked with him two men, which Peter believed were Moses and Elias,
Who appeared in glory, and spake of his decease, which he should accomplish at Jerusalem.
